intitule: Word Formation: Recognizing Suffixes and Prefixes

enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Identify the correct form of the word in brackets by adding the appropriate suffix (-tion, -sion, -ment, -ness, -ful, -less, -ive, -ous) or prefix (un-, in-, im-, dis-).

questions.texte: The government's commitment to transparency is essential for good governance. (govern)
Many young Cameroonians express their ___________ through music and art. (create)
The new policy against hate speech is a ___________ measure to prevent violence. (protect)
It is ___________ to judge someone based on their tribe or religion. (fair)
After the accident, he remained ___________ for several hours. (conscious)
The ___________ of the new school building was funded by the community. (construct)
She felt a deep sense of ___________ after volunteering at the orphanage. (happy)
His explanation was completely ___________; nobody understood the lesson. (logical)

enonce.texte:
Read the text about a student's experience during the end-of-year exams. Fill in each blank with the correct derived word from the one in brackets. Pay attention to prefixes (un-, im-, dis-) and suffixes (-tion, -ment, -ous, -less).

Exam Stress: The period of the Probatoire is often a time of great (1) ___________ (anxious) for students. To avoid being (2) ___________ (success), it is important to have a (3) ___________ (method) approach to revision. Some students feel (4) ___________ (hope) because the syllabus is wide. However, a good (5) ___________ (organize) of time can make a big (6) ___________ (differ). It is (7) ___________ (responsible) to stay up all night before the exam. The most (8) ___________ (value) advice is to stay calm and confident.

questions.texte: The period of the Probatoire is often a time of great ___________ (anxious) for students.
To avoid being ___________ (success), it is important to have a plan.
To avoid being unsuccessful, it is important to have a ___________ (method) approach to revision.
Some students feel ___________ (hope) because the syllabus is wide.
However, a good ___________ (organize) of time can make a big difference.
A good organization of time can make a big ___________ (differ).
It is ___________ (responsible) to stay up all night before the exam.
The most ___________ (value) advice is to stay calm and confident.

enonce.texte: Context: You are a youth leader in your community. During an election period, you notice that many people are sharing fake news on social media (WhatsApp, Facebook). You decide to write a short speech to educate your peers. Complete your speech by transforming the words in brackets. Use the correct prefixes (un-, im-, dis-, in-) and suffixes (-tion, -ment, -ful, -less).

questions.texte: Dear friends, the spread of (1) ___________ (inform) is a real threat to our democracy.
Sharing fake news shows a great lack of (2) ___________ (responsible).
It is (3) ___________ (moral) to manipulate facts for political gain.
Before sharing a post, always check its (4) ___________ (reliable).
Fake news can cause (5) ___________ (repair) damage to a person's reputation.
Let us be (6) ___________ (care) and think before we click the share button.
A (7) ___________ (peace) society is built on truth and transparency.
I am (8) ___________ (hope) that together we can stop this dangerous trend.
